_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 6-1 Pediatric Cardiac Arrest — General (Revised 06/06/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Pediatric Cardiac Protocol Section 6-1: Pediatric Cardiac Arrest — General — MDHHS Approved 6/6/23, Reviewed 2023
purpose: This protocol should be followed for all pediatric cardiac arrests. NOTE: Primary cardiac arrest in the pediatric patient is RARE — most arrests are secondary to respiratory failure. Maintain basic airway management techniques unless unable or ineffective. Advanced airway insertion attempts should be performed ONLY if BLS airway management is ineffective. Keep CPR interruptions to a minimum. All medications given during cardiac arrest are given IV or IO.

sections.title: Confirm Arrest and Initiate CPR
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 6-1: Pediatric Arrest — Confirm and Initiate
points: Request additional assistance as needed; initiate ALS response if available
Confirm arrest: assess for signs of normal breathing — agonal breathing is associated with cardiac arrest; check carotid or brachial pulse as age appropriate for no more than 10 seconds
Initiate or continue CPR; apply and use AED/manual defibrillator per Electrical Therapy-Procedure Protocol as soon as possible — use AED pediatric pads and settings per AED manufacturer instructions
If AED applied by BLS personnel: skip to appropriate place incorporating previous care; ALS should switch to manual defibrillator after initial AED defibrillation or place AED in manual mode
If arrest of known traumatic origin: refer to Traumatic Arrest-Treatment Protocol
If hypothermic: refer to Hypothermia/Frostbite-Treatment Protocol for warming techniques
Patients with DNR order or valid MI POST: follow DNR-Procedure Protocol or MI-POST-Procedure Protocol accordingly

sections.title: High Quality CPR and Defibrillation
points: CPR and electrical therapy consistent with current AHA guidelines
Anterior/posterior pad placement PREFERRED — use if possible and if defibrillation not delayed
Compression depth: at least 1.5 inches for infants; at least 2 inches for children (at least one third the anteroposterior diameter of the chest)
Compression rate: 100–120 per minute
Allow full chest recoil with each compression
Compression-to-ventilation ratio per current AHA pediatric guidance until advanced airway placed
Change rescuer performing compressions at least every 2 minutes to avoid fatigue
Interruptions in compressions must be less than 10 seconds
Chest Compression Fraction (CCF) should be as high as possible — ideally greater than 80%
Verify CPR quality frequently and any time rescuer changes
Avoid excessive ventilation (volume and rate)
After advanced airway placement: provide CONTINUOUS CPR without pauses for ventilation; ventilate at 20 breaths per minute (1 breath every 3 seconds) — NOTE: different from adult protocol (10/min)
Prior to advanced airway: utilize ventilation periods to visualize ECG rhythm without compression artifact

sections.title: Airway Management
points: Establish patent airway maintaining C-spine precautions if indicated — begin with BLS airway adjuncts and BVM with high flow oxygen
Ventilations with BVM (2-rescuer technique) and airway adjuncts are AT LEAST AS EFFECTIVE as endotracheal intubation in children — BVM is preferred
2-person BVM ventilation with oral airway should be standard technique; if only 2 rescuers, compressor squeezes bag while second rescuer maintains face-to-mask seal with both hands
If UNABLE to ventilate or unable to maintain patent airway: establish advanced airway per Airway Management-Procedure Protocol
Supraglottic airways are the FIRST CHOICE advanced airway for pediatrics when age-appropriate sizes are available
ALL advanced airways (including supraglottic) require ETCO2 monitoring

sections.title: Vascular Access, ETCO2, and Defibrillation
points: Start IV/IO NS or LR KVO — IO may be the FIRST CHOICE for vascular access; see Vascular Access & IV Fluid Therapy-Procedure Protocol
Continuously monitor ETCO2: ETCO2 = 0 indicates failed airway; ETCO2 < 10 mmHg — attempt to improve CPR quality (if quality good, may indicate futility state); monitor for trends

Check rhythm every 2 minutes — defibrillate according to MI-MEDIC card; if MI-MEDIC not available: initial defibrillation at 2 J/kg (or closest available energy setting); subsequent defibrillations must be at least 4 J/kg but may escalate to 10 J/kg or adult dosage
Reassess ABC's per rhythm or patient condition change — pulse checks no more than 10 seconds; if no pulse after 10 seconds, assume pulselessness and continue CPR beginning with compressions
If ROSC not achieved after three 2-minute CPR cycles AND ALS not available or delayed: contact Medical Control to discuss initiation of BLS transport while continuing high-quality CPR

sections.title: Medications
points: Administer epinephrine according to MI-MEDIC cards — initial dose should ideally be administered within 5 minutes of ALS/LALS contact of confirmed pediatric cardiac arrest
If MI-MEDIC not available — epinephrine: 1 mg/10 mL; 0.01 mg/kg (0.1 mL/kg) IV/IO; maximum dose 1 mg (10 mL); repeat every 3–5 minutes
If shockable rhythm persists: administer antidysrhythmic per MCA selection according to MI-MEDIC cards

If MI-MEDIC not available — antidysrhythmic per MCA selection: amiodarone 5 mg/kg (max single dose 300 mg) IV/IO (may repeat twice; total maximum 450 mg IV/IO) OR lidocaine 1 mg/kg IV/IO (may repeat 0.5 mg/kg twice at 5–10 minute intervals; maximum 3 doses total)

sections.title: Reversible Causes — Targeted Treatment
points: Identify and communicate to Medical Control potentially reversible causes (H's and T's): Hyper/hypokalemia (known renal failure)/other metabolic disorders; Hypothermia; Hypovolemia (including vomiting/diarrhea); Hypoxia; Hydrogen ion excess (acidosis); Toxins/overdose (beta-blocker or CCB); Tamponade; Tension pneumothorax; Thrombosis (pulmonary or coronary)
NOTE: Routine use of sodium bicarbonate and calcium chloride in cardiac arrest is NOT indicated
HYPOVOLEMIA: administer 20 mL/kg NS or LR IV/IO bolus

HYPOGLYCEMIA: check blood glucose (may be MFR skill); if blood glucose < 60 mg/dL administer dextrose per MI-MEDIC cards; if MI-MEDIC unavailable, administer dextrose 0.5 g/kg per Pediatric Altered Mental Status protocol
TENSION PNEUMOTHORAX: see Pleural Decompression-Procedure Protocol

HYPERKALEMIA (renal failure): contact Medical Control; administer calcium chloride 10% per MI-MEDIC cards; if MI-MEDIC unavailable: 20 mg/kg (0.2 mL/kg) max single dose 1 gm; FLUSH line with 20 mL NS between calcium chloride and sodium bicarbonate; administer sodium bicarbonate per MI-MEDIC; if unavailable: 1 mEq/kg IV/IO

sections.title: ROSC and Termination
points: If ROSC achieved: refer to Pediatric Return of Spontaneous Circulation-Treatment Protocol
If ROSC not achieved, continue resuscitation while contacting Medical Control

BLS/LALS: if ROSC not achieved and ALS unavailable or delayed, contact Medical Control after 20 minutes of high-quality CPR for further direction AND before initiating transport; continue high-quality CPR unless directed otherwise per Dead on Scene & Termination of Resuscitation Protocol
ALS: if ROSC not present after 30 minutes of ALS time, contact Medical Control for further direction AND before initiating transport
Continue high-quality CPR unless directed otherwise per Dead on Scene & Termination of Resuscitation Protocol

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select antidysrhythmic for persistent shockable rhythm (VF/pVT). Use MI-MEDIC for dosing when available.
options: Amiodarone 5 mg/kg IV/IO (max single dose 300 mg; may repeat twice; total max 450 mg)
Lidocaine 1 mg/kg IV/IO (may repeat 0.5 mg/kg twice at 5–10 minute intervals; maximum 3 doses total)

mca_selections_required.item: Mechanical Chest Compression Device — MCA Optional Protocol
mca_selections_required.description: MCA may authorize FDA-approved mechanical CPR device for specific settings. Must operate at manufacturer pre-set rate. See Mechanical Chest Compression Device-Procedure Protocol for age/weight requirements.

mca_selections_required.item: Impedance Threshold Device — MCA Optional Protocol
mca_selections_required.description: MCA may authorize ITD during CPR for children > 10 kg. Discontinue immediately upon ROSC.
options: Authorized for children > 10 kg per MCA Optional Protocol

key_drugs.name: Epinephrine IV/IO (Pediatric Cardiac Arrest)
key_drugs.dose: Per MI-MEDIC cards — backup: 0.01 mg/kg (0.1 mL/kg of 1 mg/10 mL solution) IV/IO; maximum dose 1 mg (10 mL); repeat every 3–5 minutes
key_drugs.indication: Pediatric cardiac arrest — all rhythms; ideally within 5 minutes of ALS/LALS contact
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC for weight-based dosing. Maximum single dose 1 mg (10 mL).

key_drugs.name: Amiodarone IV/IO (Pediatric — Shockable Rhythm)
key_drugs.dose: 5 mg/kg IV/IO (max single dose 300 mg) — may repeat twice; total maximum 450 mg IV/IO; per MI-MEDIC when available
key_drugs.indication: Persistent shockable rhythm (VF/pVT) — per MCA selection
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC for dosing. MCA must select amiodarone OR lidocaine.

key_drugs.name: Lidocaine IV/IO (Pediatric — Shockable Rhythm)
key_drugs.dose: 1 mg/kg IV/IO — may repeat 0.5 mg/kg twice at 5–10 minute intervals; maximum 3 doses total; per MI-MEDIC when available
key_drugs.indication: Persistent shockable rhythm (VF/pVT) — per MCA selection
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC for dosing. MCA must select amiodarone OR lidocaine.

key_drugs.name: Dextrose IV/IO (Pediatric Hypoglycemia Arrest)
key_drugs.dose: Per MI-MEDIC cards; backup: 0.5 g/kg per Pediatric Altered Mental Status protocol weight table
key_drugs.indication: Blood glucose < 60 mg/dL as reversible cause of arrest
key_drugs.contraindications: Concentrated D50 avoided in small children — dilute per weight table
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC for dosing. See Pediatric AMS protocol for backup weight/color zone table.

key_drugs.name: Calcium Chloride 10% IV/IO (Pediatric Hyperkalemia)
key_drugs.dose: Per MI-MEDIC cards; backup: 20 mg/kg (0.2 mL/kg); maximum single dose 1 gm — NOT routine
key_drugs.indication: Suspected or confirmed hyperkalemia (renal failure) as reversible cause — NOT routine; contact Medical Control
key_drugs.contraindications: Routine use NOT indicated; must flush line with 20 mL NS before sodium bicarbonate
key_drugs.side_effects: Tissue necrosis with extravasation; bradycardia if given too rapidly
key_drugs.notes: ALWAYS flush with 20 mL NS between calcium chloride and sodium bicarbonate. Use MI-MEDIC. Contact Medical Control.

key_drugs.name: Sodium Bicarbonate IV/IO (Pediatric)
key_drugs.dose: Per MI-MEDIC cards; backup: 1 mEq/kg IV/IO — NOT routine
key_drugs.indication: Hyperkalemia (after calcium chloride + NS flush) — NOT routine
key_drugs.contraindications: Routine use NOT indicated; give after calcium chloride with 20 mL NS flush between agents
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC for dosing. Flush line with 20 mL NS between calcium chloride and bicarbonate.

deterioration_flags: ETCO2 = 0 after advanced airway — failed airway; reassess placement immediately
ETCO2 < 10 mmHg — improve CPR quality; if quality already optimal may indicate futility state
No ROSC after 3 two-minute CPR cycles with ALS delayed — contact Medical Control re: BLS transport; continue high-quality CPR
Suspected hyperkalemia — calcium chloride per MI-MEDIC; flush; sodium bicarbonate; contact Medical Control
Suspected hypoglycemia — dextrose per MI-MEDIC or weight/color table
Persistent VF/pVT — defibrillate; antidysrhythmic per MCA selection after 3 shocks
scope_notes: CPR, AED application, BVM ventilation, basic airway adjuncts: all levels
IO access: Specialist skill — may be first choice for pediatric arrest
IV access, epinephrine, amiodarone, lidocaine, dextrose: Specialist skill

Supraglottic airway (age-appropriate size): EMT base scope; I-Gel: EMT scope at MCAs with i_gel_bls expansion; ETI: Specialist skill — only when no gag + requires sustained PPV + all BLS and SGA options have failed
ETCO2 monitoring required for all advanced airways (including supraglottic)
Calcium chloride, sodium bicarbonate: Specialist skill — not routine; hyperkalemia only
Mechanical CPR device: per MCA Optional Protocol; ITD > 10 kg: per MCA Optional Protocol
BLS/LALS: contact MC after 20 min; ALS: contact after 30 min ALS time
